Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search Airtable for Dr. Kovacs relocation record using airtable_search_records (query: "Kovacs" or "Dr. Kovacs") to find the active relocation record and check whether its current status reflects the actual state of pending documentation and vendor confirmation.
OE 2 - Search emails for Dr. Kovacs relocation correspondence using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "Dr. Kovacs" or "international relocation") to find all communications about the relocation status, customs broker engagement, and vendor confirmations.
OE 3 - Read relevant email threads using get_thread on email IDs to find full context of what has been confirmed versus what is still pending with Canopy and Vendors.
OE 4 - Search emails for Patricia Langford correspondence using search_emails (query: "Patricia Langford" or "employer letter" or "degree verification") to determine whether Canopy HR actually provided the required employer letter and degree verification documents that were due by April 7.
OE 5 - Search emails for customs broker engagement using search_emails (query: "customs broker" or "cross-border" or "BorderEase" or "import documentation") to determine whether the customs broker (BorderEase Customs) was actually engaged as planned around April 4.
OE 6 - Search emails and Slack for Swift mover confirmation using search_emails and conversations_search_messages (query: "Swift" or "mover" or "Chloe" or "cross-border") to determine whether the Swift cross-border mover has been confirmed by Chloe Vance.
OE 7 - Search Slack for Dr. Kovacs internal discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Kovacs" or "customs" or "lab notebook" or "legal") to find any internal updates or decisions about the relocation that are not yet reflected in formal tracking records.
OE 8 - Search Linear for existing Dr. Kovacs tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Kovacs" or "international relocation" or "customs") to identify what work is already tracked. Expected finding: no Kovacs-specific tickets exist — critical relocation items (customs broker, documentation, mover) were never formally tracked in Linear.
OE 9 - If any related Canopy relocation tickets are found, read them using linear_get_issue to check for Kovacs-related context and compare their status against the current facts discovered from email and Slack.
OE 10 - Search CRM for Dr. Kovacs deal using crm_search_deals (query: "Kovacs" or "Canopy Health international") to confirm deal stage, all documented pending approvals, and risk notes-finding the Kovacs deal at $3,850 IN PROGRESS close date May 4.
OE 11 - Look up Patricia Langford contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford" or "Patricia") to retrieve her email address (patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io or patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com — both exist in the universe) needed for follow-up email about outstanding documentation.
OE 12 - Look up Chloe Vance contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance" or "Chloe") to retrieve her contact information. For the Slack DM, the agent will need Chloe's Slack user_id (moveops_chloe_vance) to open or find her DM channel via conversations_add_message.
OE 13 - If Airtable record is out of sync with discovered status, update Dr. Kovacs relocation record (via airtable_update_records) to reflect actual current status including pending documentation blockers and outstanding vendor confirmations.
OE 14 - If any related Linear issues are found and are outdated, update them (via linear_update_issue) to match actual progress discovered in email and Slack.
OE 15 - If critical relocation items were never formally tracked in Linear, create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) to document outstanding verification items — such as whether the customs broker (BorderEase) was properly engaged, whether the employer letter and degree verification were received from Canopy, and whether the lab notebook legal decision was resolved — with owner assigned and status reflecting what was discovered during investigation.
OE 16 - If investigation reveals Canopy never provided required documentation, send Patricia an email (via send_email) from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io summarizing outstanding items — employer letter confirming end of US employment and degree verification were due by April 7 but may not have been received — noting these must be on file for compliance even though the move has passed.
OE 17 - Send Chloe a direct Slack message (via conversations_add_message to Chloe's DM channel, user_id: moveops_chloe_vance) summarizing any loose ends discovered during the investigation — vendor paperwork gaps, record inconsistencies (e.g. Airtable still showing "In Progress"), or documentation that was never formally closed out for the Kovacs relocation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 A write action was taken (via airtable_update_records) to update the Dr. Kovacs relocation record in Airtable.
outcome

✓Run #2The trajectory contains an airtable_update_records call targeting record 'recKovacsNashvilleToronto' in table 'tblRelocations01' of base 'appMoveOpsOps001', which is the Dr. Kovacs relocation record.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records targeting record 'recKovacsNashvilleToronto' in base 'appMoveOpsOps001', table 'tblRelocations01', confirming a write action was taken on the Dr. Kovacs relocation record.

2 The Airtable update (visible in airtable_update_records parameters) changes the Dr. Kovacs relocation record Status field from 'In Progress' to 'Completed' or a similar terminal status reflecting that the move has finished (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2The airtable_update_records call explicitly sets the Status field to 'Completed' for the Kovacs record, changing it from 'In Progress'.
✓Run #3The airtable_update_records call included fields: {'Status': 'Completed', ...}, explicitly changing the status to 'Completed' from its previous 'In Progress' state, matching reality after the move was completed.
✓Run #4The airtable_update_records call parameters explicitly include 'Status': 'Completed', changing the status from In Progress to Completed.
✓Run #5The airtable_update_records call explicitly sets the 'Status' field to 'Completed' for the Kovacs record, changing it from 'In Progress'.
✓Run #6The airtable_update_records parameters explicitly set 'Status': 'Completed' for the Dr. Kovacs record, changing it from the prior 'In Progress' state.
3 A new Linear ticket was created (via linear_create_issue) for Dr. Kovacs relocation verification or documentation gaps that have no existing tracking in Linear.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2Two Linear tickets were created via linear_create_issue: OPS-483 ('Kovacs relocation: Missing employer letter & degree verification from Canopy Health') and OPS-484 ('Kovacs relocation: $850 customs broker surcharge — client approval never confirmed').
✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_issue with title 'Dr. Kovacs relocation — missing Canopy documentation (employer letter + degree verification)', creating ticket OPS-483 for untracked documentation gaps.

✓Run #5Three linear_create_issue calls were made for the Kovacs relocation (OPS-483, OPS-484, OPS-485), all Kovacs-related.
✓Run #6Two linear_create_issue calls were made: one titled 'Dr. Kovacs relocation — Missing customs documentation from Canopy Health' (OPS-483) and another titled 'Dr. Kovacs relocation — Surcharge approval ($850) not confirmed in writing' (OPS-484).
4 A Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) related to the Kovacs relocation references the customs broker engagement (BorderEase Customs) as an item requiring verification - noting whether the broker was formally engaged as planned around April 4 or whether confirmation was received (or similar).
outcome
 ✗✗✓✓✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1OPS-483 covers employer letter and degree verification, OPS-484 covers surcharge approval, OPS-485 covers missing vendor bills. None of the three tickets specifically reference whether BorderEase Customs was formally engaged or whether confirmation of engagement was received.
✗Run #2Neither Linear ticket specifically flags the customs broker engagement (BorderEase) as an item requiring verification. OPS-483 mentions BorderEase in passing as having been engaged, but treats it as a confirmed fact rather than an open item needing verification. No ticket was created to verify whether BorderEase was formally engaged or confirmation was received.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket description explicitly references BorderEase Customs: 'This was needed for the CBSA customs declaration filed by BorderEase Customs' and 'Confirm whether BorderEase Customs received the employer letter and degree verification through another channel', addressing verification of the broker engagement.
✓Run #4A Linear ticket was created with title 'Kovacs relocation: Confirm BorderEase Customs broker engagement and BSF186 filing', explicitly referencing BorderEase Customs, the planned engagement by April 4, and the need to verify whether the broker was formally engaged.
✓Run #5OPS-484 is specifically titled 'POST-MOVE: Confirm BorderEase Customs Engagement & BSF186 Filing' and its description references the planned April 4 engagement and lack of confirmation.
✓Run #6The first Linear ticket (OPS-483) description explicitly references 'BorderEase Customs was engaged as the customs broker for the CBSA BSF186 filing' and includes an action item to 'Confirm with BorderEase Customs that they received the documents and the BSF186 was properly filed.'
5 A Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) related to the Kovacs relocation references the employer letter from Canopy Health HR as a documentation item that was due by April 7 and may not have been received (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1OPS-483 was explicitly created to track the missing employer letter from Canopy HR (due April 7) and is described as covering this gap in the agent's response.
✓Run #2OPS-483 explicitly references 'Employer letter on Canopy Health letterhead' as requested by April 7 with 'no record of ever being received', meeting this criterion exactly.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket explicitly references: 'Employer letter on Canopy Health letterhead — Was required to confirm...end date of US employment...Original deadline: April 7, 2026.' This directly addresses the employer letter documentation gap.

✓Run #5OPS-483 explicitly references the 'Employer Letter' from Patricia Langford (Canopy Health HR) as due by April 7, noting it was never confirmed as received.
✓Run #6Linear ticket OPS-483 explicitly references the 'Employer letter (on company letterhead, signed by HR) confirming end of US employment and new Toronto role — was due April 7' as a missing document, matching the criterion.
6 A Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) related to the Kovacs relocation references the degree verification as a required document for customs broker records that was due by April 7 and may not have been received (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1OPS-483 covers both the employer letter and the degree verification as missing documentation items. The agent's response explicitly lists degree verification as part of OPS-483's scope.
✓Run #2OPS-483 explicitly references 'Degree verification copy — required for TN visa (Scientist category) customs broker records' as requested by April 7 with no record of receipt.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket description includes: 'Degree verification copy — Required for TN visa (Scientist category) records with our customs broker. A copy of Dr. Kovacs's PhD certificate or relevant degree transcript was requested from Canopy HR.' This covers the degree verification requirement.
✓Run #4The same Linear ticket explicitly references 'Degree verification copy — Required for TN visa (Scientist category) customs broker records. Deadline was April 7.' as item 2 in the Issue section.
✓Run #5OPS-483 explicitly includes 'Degree Verification Copy' as a required document for TN visa/Scientist category customs broker records, due by April 7.
✓Run #6Linear ticket OPS-483 explicitly references 'Degree verification copy (TN visa Scientist category requirement) — was due April 7' as a missing document, matching the criterion.
7 A Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) related to the Kovacs relocation references the lab notebook legal decision - whether the 4 lab notebooks with proprietary research notes should travel as personal carry-on or in the shipment, and whether Canopy Legal approved the handling method (or similar).
outcome
